body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"decompose: create child \"config: stop defaulting DDx library path to project plugin payload\": context canceled","fingerprint":"7c518ad7ec09569d2bebf32d5d9d25d71202c751fac9a601b37c3f537c661348","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"decomposition_error_best_effort","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.decomposition_error_best_effort","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"7c518ad7ec09569d2bebf32d5d9d25d71202c751fac9a601b37c3f537c661348"}

{"ac_map":[{"parent_ac":"1. TestInitProject_MaterializesBuiltinAdaptersWithoutPluginPayload still passes and is extended or paired with a new TestDefaultDDxPackageDoesNotRequireProjectPayload proving plain ddx init/plugin sync leaves .ddx/plugins/ddx absent while .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx work.","coverage":"init child AC1, AC2, and AC3 cover preserving TestInitProject_MaterializesBuiltinAdaptersWithoutPluginPayload, adding TestDefaultDDxPackageDoesNotRequireProjectPayload, proving .ddx/plugins/ddx absence, and verifying .agents/skills/ddx plus .claude/skills/ddx usability."},{"parent_ac":"2. New or updated registry/config tests prove DefaultNewConfig/ApplyDefaults no longer teach .ddx/plugins/ddx as the normal project payload path for the built-in DDx package.","coverage":"config child AC1, AC2, and AC3 cover DefaultNewConfig and ApplyDefaults; registry child AC1 and AC3 cover the registry side of no longer teaching .ddx/plugins/ddx as the normal built-in package path."},{"parent_ac":"3. Registry/defaultplugin tests distinguish compatibility/local-overlay install.root metadata from marketplace/cache-backed generated adapter behavior and stop asserting .ddx/plugins/ddx as the forward install target.","coverage":"registry child AC1, AC2, and AC3 cover defaultplugin metadata, embedded fallback resolution, local-overlay compatibility, and removal of .ddx/plugins/ddx as the forward install target."},{"parent_ac":"4. CLI help and frontend/e2e fixture docs no longer instruct users to rely on .ddx/plugins/ddx for normal marketplace installs.","coverage":"docs child AC1, AC2, and AC3 cover CLI help, frontend/e2e fixture documentation, and limiting remaining .ddx/plugins/ddx mentions to local-overlay or legacy compatibility context."},{"parent_ac":"5. `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./internal/registry/... ./internal/config/... ./cmd/... ./internal/server/frontend/...` passes.","coverage":"config child AC4, registry child AC4, init child AC4, and docs child AC4 all preserve the full parent go test gate."},{"parent_ac":"6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.","coverage":"config child AC5, registry child AC5, init child AC5, and docs child AC5 all preserve the lefthook gate."}],"child_ids":["ddx-dc959108","ddx-4ecebef2","ddx-ff032e16","ddx-7f5092f2"],"rationale":"The parent bead spans four independently executable surfaces: config defaults, registry/defaultplugin metadata, init/plugin-sync behavior, and user-facing docs/help. Splitting by surface keeps each child small enough to implement and test in isolation while preserving the parent non-scope constraints, governing artifact, parent reference, and full verification gates on every child."}
summary: decomposed into ddx-dc959108, ddx-4ecebef2, ddx-ff032e16, ddx-7f5092f2
